description: Trigger discrimination for the client offline sync ENGINE (this skill) vs the offline/optimistic UX STATES it feeds (edge-state-ux-designer), the server/distributed cache (caching-strategy-designer), and live ONLINE server→client push (realtime-subscription-architect — the in-batch seam, pinned both ways).

cases.prompt: Design the offline write queue, optimistic apply with rollback on reject, version-based conflict detection and a resolution policy, and background sync that reconciles with the server without duplicating or losing data.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary) — write queue, optimistic apply/rollback, conflict detection/resolution, and reconciliation are the sync ENGINE. Not the UX of those states, not a server cache, not online push.
cases.reason: The offline data layer's engine — queue, conflict handling, reconciliation — is this skill; it hands the state rendering to edge-state-ux-designer and may sync over the realtime transport.

cases.prompt: Design how we PRESENT offline and optimistic states to users: the 'saved locally, will sync' badge, the syncing/synced/failed indicators, and how a rolled-back change is shown after the server rejects it.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary, reverse) — presenting offline/optimistic/rollback STATES to the user is UX. The engine that produces those states is separate.
cases.reason: Rendering the offline/optimistic/error states is edge-state-ux-designer. This skill is the sync engine BEHIND those states; the two compose.

cases.prompt: Design our server-side and distributed caching: what to cache at which layer, TTLs, cache keys, and invalidation when the source data changes.
cases.reason: Server-side/distributed cache design (what/where/TTL/invalidation) is caching-strategy-designer. A client on-device offline store that queues writes is not a server cache.

cases.prompt: When a shared record changes, push it live to every online client viewing it over WebSocket, authorized at subscribe time, with reconnect/replay and presence.
cases.note: IN-BATCH SEAM (reciprocal of realtime-subscription-architect's offline-sync case) — live server→client delivery while ONLINE, subscribe-time authz, and presence are realtime. The offline write queue and conflict resolution are not in the ask.
cases.reason: Live online push, subscribe-time authorization, and presence are realtime-subscription-architect. The two compose (realtime can be the sync transport) but offline-first owns write-while-offline, optimistic rollback, and conflict resolution.
